content.text: The best Absorb is elsewhere.

The Dancer is light blades, light armor, and the conviction that the best Absorb is elsewhere. Its lanes are the ones the math built for light weapons: flesh-hunting, condition delivery, crit-fishing, shell-grinding — and not being there when the answer comes back.
Two branches hold the lane. Tempest is the storm of edges: more attacks, sharper riders. Quicksilver is the untouchable: Evasion that must keep moving to exist.
Contractor has declined standard ballistic coverage. Liability for incoming fire transfers to the contractor's footwork, effective immediately. We admire the confidence.

content.stat_lean: Reflex and Edge — Evasion is built from both (the formula — see Attacks & the Shell Clock).
content.theme: Dual Swing's home tree: flurries, riders, and conditional Evasion that must keep moving.
content.design: One more attack per turn, paid in Evasion — and a ceiling that makes heavies stop aiming.

content.text: The Engine — One More Attack, Paid in Evasion
This is Dual Swing's home tree. The rule itself — and Titan Grip, the heavy-pair procedure — lives on Dual Wield & Titan Grip; this page sells the discounts.

Baseline, simmed: a dual-knife turn reaches 6 attacks against a single knife's 5 — 21.7 damage per turn against flesh versus 18.2. Roughly one free attack per turn, with the Evasion penalty as the price tag. The tree escalates from there: a Twin Tempest turn reaches 7 attacks and 25.4 against flesh — about +40% over single-wield — wearing the full penalty twice.
Heavy pairs are the other lane, and Dancer discounts never apply to them (see Dual Wield & Titan Grip). This lane is grace; that lane is chrome.

The Dancer's bill is printed on every flurry: Dual Swing's Evasion penalty is the cost of the extra attack, and the Quicksilver bonuses only pay while you move. The tree pays in exposure — the answer comes back during the window you bought the swings in.
What the Evasion Ceiling Buys

The Dancer owns the Evasion ceiling: +3, plus the capstone's removal of the light-armor Evasion cap (armor caps — see Armor; the Evasion formula — see Attacks & the Shell Clock). What the ladder buys, in expected damage per incoming attack:

Past EV 11, heavy weapons stop aiming and start praying — and prayer auto-hits. The axe's numbers don't improve from EV 11 to EV 14 because by then it can only crit, and crits don't care (crits are EV-proof — see Graze & Crits). Heavy crit-fishers are the Dancer's designed predator.
Knives hunt dancers. d12 accuracy keeps 33% contact at EV 14 — the mirror match is the only fair fight in the building.
The crit floor, printed: a five-attack knife enemy still lands about 2.9 damage per turn on an EV 14 Dancer through natural maxes alone. Untouchable, not invincible.
Counterplay, Printed
Crits — EV-proof by rule (see Graze & Crits).
Flanking — the Dancer has no anti-flank augment; positioning is the whole job. “Cannot be flanked” lives in Fortress (Watchman Optic). The Dancer dodges dice, not geometry.
Grapples — contested Checks never look at Evasion. Athletics is the dodge-tank's tax.
Suppression and cluster patterns — no attack roll, no dodge.
Attrition — graze chip from accurate enemies (see Graze & Crits).
The Wall — the Fortress capstone deletes the free shift entirely; that arms race is printed in both trees.
the fast ones always skip wrestling practice. the floor never does.
The Catalog
Install costs follow the standard ladder (Trees Overview). Nineteen augments; Tier 5 means choosing ten of them. Capstones are marked.

augments.name: Gyro Threads
augments.effect: While wearing Weave-class armor or lighter, +1 band of movement per turn.

content.text: Tempest Branch — The Storm of Edges

augments.name: Balanced Tang
augments.effect: Your off-hand weapon counts one weight lighter for Dual Swing penalties.

augments.name: Off-hand Sync
augments.effect: Dual Swing's Evasion penalty is reduced by 2 (minimum 0).

augments.name: Razor Tempo
augments.effect: Your fourth and later attacks each turn gain +1 accuracy. Counts toward the accuracy cap.

augments.name: Serration Memory
augments.effect: Consecutive hits on the same target in one turn deal +1 damage (max +2). The grind, sharpened.

augments.name: Twin Tempest
augments.effect: You may Dual Swing twice per turn — seven-attack turns, wearing the full Evasion penalty twice.

augments.name: Venom Channels
augments.effect: Once per turn, one weapon rider applies even on a graze — a priced exception to the graze rule (see Graze & Crits).

augments.name: Crit Harmonics
augments.effect: Your light blades (1–2 AP) gain +1 crit beat.

augments.name: Blur of Edges
augments.effect: The first light-blade kill each turn refunds 1 AP.

augments.name: Bladestorm
augments.effect: Capstone. Once per fight, 4 AP: one attack against every enemy in your Adjacent cluster, and Dual Swing penalties don't apply this turn.

content.text: Quicksilver Branch — The Untouchable

augments.name: Heel Turn
augments.effect: When an enemy enters your Adjacent cluster, you may immediately take your free Adjacent-to-Close shift.

augments.name: Slip Servos
augments.effect: +1 Evasion while in Weave or lighter.

augments.name: Ghost Step
augments.effect: Your free Adjacent-to-Close shift never triggers reaction attacks. The Wall deletes the free shift entirely — that arms race is intended and printed in both trees.

augments.name: Quicksilver Frame
augments.effect: +1 Evasion in any turn you moved at least one band. The dancer must dance.

augments.name: Slipstream
augments.effect: 1 AP: until your next turn, reaction attacks triggered by your movement roll accuracy twice and take the lower.

augments.name: Matador's Read
augments.effect: When a melee attack misses you, +1 accuracy on your next attack against that attacker. Counts toward the accuracy cap.

augments.name: Afterimage
augments.effect: Once per fight, when an attack hits you, force a complete re-roll.

augments.name: Mercury Burst
augments.effect: 2 AP: move two bands, ignoring difficult terrain and all reaction triggers.

augments.name: Untouchable
augments.effect: Capstone. +1 Evasion — the Dancer ceiling, +3 — and Weave-or-lighter armor no longer caps your Evasion.

content.text: Pairings
+Fury: more swings, more Carnage triggers — but light blades starve Wrath's damage-taken feed, and Dual Swing's Evasion penalty argues with Pain Converter's whole lifestyle.
+Shadow: the alpha-strike Opener into flurry cleanup is the obvious marriage. The Shadow dodges turns, the Dancer dodges dice — the lanes stay distinct.
+Leech (Parasite): Siphon Fangs on six-attack turns is a drip-feed sustain build; the grapple requirement fights the Dancer's instincts — honest tension.
Notice: evasion percentile achievements do not constitute invulnerability. Contractors are reminded that natural maximums occur at the printed rate regardless of morale.
untouchable is a streak, not a property. plan for the night the streak ends.
